mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 300 indicates elevated inflation, prompting central bank tightening and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 300 despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and PAYEMS fails to predict SPX returns with a 3-year rolling Rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising FEDFUNDS and elevated UNRATE signal reduced liquidity and higher borrowing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of periods where both FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and UNRATE > 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Continued growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict SPX returns with a 1-week rolling Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 100k signals labor market strength, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer and business demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through increased economic activity.
failure_condition: The joint effect of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict SPX returns with a 3-year rolling Rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In high unemployment regimes (UNRATE > 4.5%), weak labor market conditions reduce consumer spending and corporate profits, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%.
